 {"id":"bd-10i.4.3","title":"Implement Presenter with state-tracked ANSI emission","description":"# Implement Presenter with state-tracked ANSI emission

## Objective
Implement the core ANSI emitter that transforms buffer diffs into terminal output with minimal bytes.

## Technical Design

### State Tracking
Track current terminal state to avoid redundant sequences:
- current_style: Option<CellStyle> (fg, bg, attrs)
- current_link: Option<u32> (link ID)
- cursor_x, cursor_y: u16 (cursor position)

### Structure
pub struct Presenter<W: Write> {
    writer: BufWriter<W>,    // 64KB buffer
    current_style: Option<CellStyle>,
    current_link: Option<u32>,
    cursor_x: u16,
    cursor_y: u16,
    capabilities: TerminalCapabilities,
}

#[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
struct CellStyle {
    fg: PackedRgba,
    bg: PackedRgba,
    attrs: StyleFlags,
}

### Implementation

impl<W: Write> Presenter<W> {
    pub fn new(writer: W, capabilities: TerminalCapabilities) -> Self {
        Self {
            writer: BufWriter::with_capacity(64 * 1024, writer),
            current_style: None,
            current_link: None,
            cursor_x: 0,
            cursor_y: 0,
            capabilities,
        }
    }

    /// Present a frame using diff
    pub fn present(
        &mut self,
        buffer: &Buffer,
        diff: &BufferDiff,
        pool: &GraphemePool,
        links: &LinkRegistry,
    ) -> io::Result<()> {
        // Begin synchronized output
        if self.capabilities.sync_output {
            self.writer.write_all(b"\x1b[?2026h")?;
        }

        // Emit diff using run grouping
        self.emit_diff(buffer, diff, pool, links)?;

        // Reset style at end (clean state)
        self.writer.write_all(b"\x1b[0m")?;
        self.current_style = None;

        // Close any open link
        if self.current_link.is_some() {
            self.writer.write_all(b"\x1b]8;;\x1b\\")?;
            self.current_link = None;
        }

        // End synchronized output
        if self.capabilities.sync_output {
            self.writer.write_all(b"\x1b[?2026l")?;
        }

        self.writer.flush()
    }

    fn emit_diff(...) -> io::Result<()> {
        for run in diff.runs(buffer.width()) {
            // Single cursor move per run
            self.move_cursor_to(run.x0, run.y)?;

            // Emit cells (cursor advances naturally)
            for x in run.x0..=run.x1 {
                let cell = buffer.get_unchecked(x, run.y);
                self.emit_cell(cell, pool, links)?;
            }
        }
        Ok(())
    }

    fn emit_cell(...) -> io::Result<()> {
        if cell.is_continuation() { return Ok(()); }

        self.emit_style_changes(cell)?;
        self.emit_link_changes(cell, links)?;
        self.emit_content(cell, pool)?;

        self.cursor_x += cell.width() as u16;
        Ok(())
    }

    fn emit_style_changes(&mut self, cell: &Cell) -> io::Result<()> {
        // v1: Reset+apply strategy (per ADR-002)
        // Emit SGR 0 then apply all attributes
        // Optimization: skip if style unchanged
    }

    fn emit_link_changes(&mut self, cell: &Cell, registry: &LinkRegistry) -> io::Result<()> {
        // Open/close OSC 8 links as needed
    }

    fn emit_content(&mut self, cell: &Cell, pool: &GraphemePool) -> io::Result<()> {
        // Emit char or grapheme from pool
    }

    fn move_cursor_to(&mut self, x: u16, y: u16) -> io::Result<()> {
        if self.cursor_x == x && self.cursor_y == y { return Ok(()); }
        write!(self.writer, "\x1b[{};{}H", y + 1, x + 1)?;
        self.cursor_x = x;
        self.cursor_y = y;
        Ok(())
    }
}

## ANSI Sequences Reference
- Cursor position: CSI row ; col H (1-indexed)
- SGR reset: CSI 0 m
- SGR attributes: CSI n ; n ; ... m
- True color fg: CSI 38 ; 2 ; r ; g ; b m
- True color bg: CSI 48 ; 2 ; r ; g ; b m
- OSC 8 open: OSC 8 ; ; url ST
- OSC 8 close: OSC 8 ; ; ST
- Sync begin: CSI ? 2026 h
- Sync end: CSI ? 2026 l

## Tests Required
- [ ] present() produces valid ANSI
- [ ] Style tracking avoids redundant SGR
- [ ] Link tracking opens/closes correctly
- [ ] Cursor moves are minimized via runs
- [ ] Empty diff produces no cell output
- [ ] Sync output wrapped when supported

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Terminal-model tests pass
- [ ] PTY tests show correct output
- [ ] Single write per frame (buffered)
- [ ] No style/link leaks after present","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:14:16.508100183Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T00:24:18.929987975Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-10i.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.2.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:22:00.259710385Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-10i.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:14:16.508100183Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-10i.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:21:59.992602219Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-10i.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3ky.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T00:24:18.929949984Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-10i.4.4","title":"Implement TerminalCapabilities detection","description":"# Implement TerminalCapabilities detection

## Objective
Implement environment-based capability detection for adapting to different terminals.

## Detection Logic Summary

### Color Support
true_color = \!NO_COLOR && (
    COLORTERM in [truecolor, 24bit] ||
    TERM contains [kitty, wezterm, alacritty, ghostty] ||
    TERM_PROGRAM in [iTerm.app, WezTerm, Alacritty, Ghostty]
)

colors_256 = true_color || TERM contains 256color

### Synchronized Output (DEC 2026)
sync_output = TERM_PROGRAM in [WezTerm, Alacritty, Ghostty] ||
              TERM contains kitty ||
              KITTY_WINDOW_ID set

### OSC 8 Hyperlinks
osc8_hyperlinks = \!NO_COLOR && (
    TERM_PROGRAM in [iTerm.app, WezTerm, Ghostty] ||
    TERM contains kitty
)

### Multiplexer Detection
in_tmux = TMUX env var set
in_screen = STY env var set
in_zellij = ZELLIJ env var set

### Input Features
kitty_keyboard = TERM contains kitty || KITTY_WINDOW_ID set
focus_events = true (widely supported)
bracketed_paste = true (widely supported)
mouse_sgr = true (widely supported)

### Scroll Region (DECSTBM)
scroll_region = TERM \!= dumb

### OSC 52 Clipboard
osc52_clipboard = \!in_any_mux && TERM \!= dumb
(Security restricted in mux environments)

## Environment Variables Used
- NO_COLOR: Disable color (de-facto standard)
- COLORTERM: Color capability hint
- TERM: Terminal type
- TERM_PROGRAM: Terminal application name
- TMUX: tmux socket path (indicates tmux)
- STY: screen session name (indicates screen)
- ZELLIJ: Zellij session
- KITTY_WINDOW_ID: Kitty window identifier

## Implementation
impl TerminalCapabilities {
    pub fn detect() -> Self {
        let no_color = std::env::var('NO_COLOR').is_ok();
        let colorterm = std::env::var('COLORTERM').unwrap_or_default();
        let term = std::env::var('TERM').unwrap_or_default();
        let term_program = std::env::var('TERM_PROGRAM').unwrap_or_default();

        let in_tmux = std::env::var('TMUX').is_ok();
        let in_screen = std::env::var('STY').is_ok();
        let in_zellij = std::env::var('ZELLIJ').is_ok();

        // ... detection logic ...

        Self { ... }
    }

    pub fn basic() -> Self {
        // Minimal fallback: no advanced features
    }

    pub fn in_any_mux(&self) -> bool {
        self.in_tmux || self.in_screen || self.in_zellij
    }
}

## Tests Required
- [ ] NO_COLOR disables color features
- [ ] COLORTERM=truecolor enables true_color
- [ ] Known terminals detected correctly
- [ ] Multiplexer detection works
- [ ] basic() returns conservative defaults

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All common terminals detected correctly
- [ ] Conservative fallbacks for unknown
- [ ] Tests cover major scenarios","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:14:35.007795448Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T07:10:25.724243465Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T07:10:25.724223417Z","close_reason":"Detection logic fully implemented in terminal_capabilities.rs with all specified checks","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-10i.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.2.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:22:00.519607715Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-10i.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:14:35.007795448Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-10i.5","title":"Phase 3: Input System","description":"# Phase 3: Input System

## Purpose
Implement robust input parsing that handles terminal input sequences while protecting against DoS attacks.

## Event Types
- Key(KeyEvent): code, modifiers, kind (Press/Repeat/Release)
- Mouse(MouseEvent): kind, x, y, modifiers
- Resize { width, height }
- Paste(PasteEvent): text, bracketed flag
- Focus(bool): gained/lost

## Parser State Machine
States: Ground, Escape, Csi, CsiParam, Osc, Dcs, Ss3

### DoS Protection (CRITICAL)
- max_csi_len: 256 bytes (prevents CSI bomb)
- max_osc_len: 4096 bytes
- max_dcs_len: 4096 bytes
- max_paste_len: 1MB (configurable)
- Parser must remain linear time for worst-case inputs
- No panics on malformed sequences

## Protocols to Support
1. Standard ASCII + UTF-8
2. CSI sequences (arrows, function keys, modifiers)
3. SS3 sequences (alternate function keys)
4. SGR mouse protocol (1006) for precise coordinates
5. Bracketed paste mode (200~/201~)
6. Focus events (CSI I / CSI O)
7. Optional: Kitty keyboard protocol (feature-gated)

## Event Coalescing (Recommended)
- Mouse move: keep latest position per frame
- Resize: keep latest size per tick
- Key repeat: optionally surface KeyEventKind::Repeat if kitty protocol enabled

## Exit Criteria
- Parser passes fuzzing with random byte streams
- Deterministic tests for all key/mouse/paste/resize sequences
- Hard bounds on sequence lengths enforced
- No panics on any input

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All supported protocols decode into canonical Events (lossless for supported sequences).
- [ ] Hard caps prevent unbounded memory/time (DoS resistance).
- [ ] Fuzzing runs continuously in CI (or as a separate job) and remains green.
- [ ] PTY tests prove bracketed paste/mouse/focus modes are enabled/disabled correctly via RAII.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:05:35.653437996Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-31T23:26:13.403429268Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-10i.5","depends_on_id":"bd-10i","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:05:35.653437996Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-10i.5","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:21:21.059758726Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-10i.5.1","title":"Implement InputParser state machine","description":"# Implement InputParser state machine

## Objective
Implement robust terminal input parsing with DoS protection.

## Parser States
enum ParserState {
    Ground,    // Normal character input
    Escape,    // After ESC
    Csi,       // After ESC [
    CsiParam,  // Collecting CSI parameters
    Osc,       // After ESC ]
    Dcs,       // After ESC P
    Ss3,       // After ESC O
}

## Transitions
Ground:
  ESC (0x1B) -> Escape
  0x00 -> Key(Null, Ctrl)
  0x01-0x1A -> Key(Ctrl+A..Z)
  0x7F -> Key(Backspace)
  0x80+ -> UTF-8 sequence
  other -> Key(Char)

Escape:
  [ -> Csi
  O -> Ss3
  ] -> Osc
  P -> Dcs
  other -> Key(Alt+char) + Ground

Csi:
  0-9;:<=>? -> CsiParam
  A-Za-z~ -> parse + Ground

CsiParam:
  0-9;: -> stay
  A-Za-z~Mm -> parse + Ground

Ss3:
  A-S -> function key + Ground

Osc:
  BEL (0x07) -> complete + Ground
  ESC \\ -> complete + Ground

## DoS Protection Limits
const MAX_CSI_LEN: usize = 256;   // Prevent CSI bomb
const MAX_OSC_LEN: usize = 4096;
const MAX_DCS_LEN: usize = 4096;
const MAX_PASTE_LEN: usize = 1024 * 1024;  // 1MB

If limit exceeded: abandon sequence, return to Ground.

## Special Sequences

### Bracketed Paste
CSI 200 ~ -> start paste mode
CSI 201 ~ -> end paste mode, emit Paste event

### Focus Events
CSI I -> Focus(true)
CSI O -> Focus(false)

### SGR Mouse (1006)
CSI < params M -> mouse down
CSI < params m -> mouse up
params: button ; x ; y

### Arrow Keys
CSI A -> Up
CSI B -> Down
CSI C -> Right
CSI D -> Left

### Function Keys
Ss3 P -> F1
Ss3 Q -> F2
etc.

CSI 15 ~ -> F5
CSI 17 ~ -> F6
etc.

## Implementation
pub struct InputParser {
    state: ParserState,
    buffer: Vec<u8>,
    paste_buffer: Vec<u8>,
    in_paste: bool,
    // Limits
    max_csi_len: usize,
    max_osc_len: usize,
    max_paste_len: usize,
}

impl InputParser {
    pub fn new() -> Self { ... }

    pub fn parse(&mut self, input: &[u8]) -> Vec<Event> {
        let mut events = Vec::new();
        for &byte in input {
            if let Some(event) = self.process_byte(byte) {
                events.push(event);
            }
        }
        events
    }
}

## Tests Required
- [ ] ASCII characters parsed correctly
- [ ] Arrow keys produce correct events
- [ ] Function keys F1-F12
- [ ] Modifiers (Ctrl, Alt, Shift)
- [ ] Mouse events decoded correctly
- [ ] Bracketed paste collected
- [ ] Focus events detected
- [ ] DoS limits enforced
- [ ] Invalid sequences don't panic

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] Fuzz testing passes
- [ ] No panics on any input
- [ ] Linear time complexity","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:14:55.632491690Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-31T22:22:05.373599141Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-10i.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:22:05.373564095Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-10i.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:14:55.632491690Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-10i.5.1","title":"Implement InputParser state machine","description":"# Implement InputParser state machine

## Objective
Implement robust terminal input parsing with DoS protection.

## Parser States
enum ParserState {
    Ground,    // Normal character input
    Escape,    // After ESC
    Csi,       // After ESC [
    CsiParam,  // Collecting CSI parameters
    Osc,       // After ESC ]
    Dcs,       // After ESC P
    Ss3,       // After ESC O
}

## Transitions
Ground:
  ESC (0x1B) -> Escape
  0x00 -> Key(Null, Ctrl)
  0x01-0x1A -> Key(Ctrl+A..Z)
  0x7F -> Key(Backspace)
  0x80+ -> UTF-8 sequence
  other -> Key(Char)

Escape:
  [ -> Csi
  O -> Ss3
  ] -> Osc
  P -> Dcs
  other -> Key(Alt+char) + Ground

Csi:
  0-9;:<=>? -> CsiParam
  A-Za-z~ -> parse + Ground

CsiParam:
  0-9;: -> stay
  A-Za-z~Mm -> parse + Ground

Ss3:
  A-S -> function key + Ground

Osc:
  BEL (0x07) -> complete + Ground
  ESC \\ -> complete + Ground

## DoS Protection Limits
const MAX_CSI_LEN: usize = 256;   // Prevent CSI bomb
const MAX_OSC_LEN: usize = 4096;
const MAX_DCS_LEN: usize = 4096;
const MAX_PASTE_LEN: usize = 1024 * 1024;  // 1MB

If limit exceeded: abandon sequence, return to Ground.

## Special Sequences

### Bracketed Paste
CSI 200 ~ -> start paste mode
CSI 201 ~ -> end paste mode, emit Paste event

### Focus Events
CSI I -> Focus(true)
CSI O -> Focus(false)

### SGR Mouse (1006)
CSI < params M -> mouse down
CSI < params m -> mouse up
params: button ; x ; y

### Arrow Keys
CSI A -> Up
CSI B -> Down
CSI C -> Right
CSI D -> Left

### Function Keys
Ss3 P -> F1
Ss3 Q -> F2
etc.

CSI 15 ~ -> F5
CSI 17 ~ -> F6
etc.

## Implementation
pub struct InputParser {
    state: ParserState,
    buffer: Vec<u8>,
    paste_buffer: Vec<u8>,
    in_paste: bool,
    // Limits
    max_csi_len: usize,
    max_osc_len: usize,
    max_paste_len: usize,
}

impl InputParser {
    pub fn new() -> Self { ... }

    pub fn parse(&mut self, input: &[u8]) -> Vec<Event> {
        let mut events = Vec::new();
        for &byte in input {
            if let Some(event) = self.process_byte(byte) {
                events.push(event);
            }
        }
        events
    }
}

## Tests Required
- [ ] ASCII characters parsed correctly
- [ ] Arrow keys produce correct events
- [ ] Function keys F1-F12
- [ ] Modifiers (Ctrl, Alt, Shift)
- [ ] Mouse events decoded correctly
- [ ] Bracketed paste collected
- [ ] Focus events detected
- [ ] DoS limits enforced
- [ ] Invalid sequences don't panic

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] Fuzz testing passes
- [ ] No panics on any input
- [ ] Linear time complexity","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:14:55.632491690Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T07:10:44.143604185Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-10i.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:22:05.373564095Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-10i.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:14:55.632491690Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-10i.6","title":"Phase 4: Styling (Split Responsibilities)","description":"# Phase 4: Styling (Split Responsibilities)

## Purpose
Implement a style system that separates concerns: renderer-facing CellStyle is tiny and fast, while higher-level style (theme/semantic colors) resolves into CellStyle before drawing.

## Architecture Decision
Style is split by responsibility:
- CellStyle: tiny, renderer-facing (packed fg/bg/attrs/link-id)
- FullStyle: higher-level with all properties + explicit tracking via bitflags
- Theme: semantic color slots + presets

## Components

### StyleFlags (8 bits)
BOLD, DIM, ITALIC, UNDERLINE, BLINK, REVERSE, STRIKETHROUGH, HIDDEN

### Props Bitflags (explicit property tracking)
Tracks which properties are explicitly set for proper inheritance.
Unset properties inherit from parent style.

### Color System
- AdaptiveColor: can resolve differently for light/dark themes
- ColorProfile detection: Ascii, Ansi (16), Ansi256 (256), TrueColor (24-bit)
- Color downgrade: TrueColor -> 256 -> 16 -> ASCII
- NO_COLOR environment variable respected

### Border System
- Presets: Rounded, Square, Double, Heavy, Ascii, Hidden
- Custom borders supported
- All corner and edge characters defined

### Markup Parser (Optional Layer)
- Regex-based parser for Rich-style markup: [bold red]text[/]
- Escape sequences: \[ becomes literal [
- Style stack for proper nesting
- Feature-gated, not part of kernel

## Exit Criteria
- Deterministic style merge with explicit masks
- Theme resolution documented and tested
- Markup parsing correct under tests (feature-gated)
- Color downgrade works for all profile levels

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Style merge semantics are deterministic and documented (no hidden precedence).
- [ ] Color downgrade is correct and tested across profiles.
- [ ] Border presets are defined and used consistently by widgets.
- [ ] Markup parsing is optional and cannot destabilize the kernel.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:05:49.668320221Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-31T23:26:30.002720740Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-10i.6","depends_on_id":"bd-10i","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:05:49.668320221Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-10i.6","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:21:21.317323021Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-10i.7","title":"Phase 5: Layout + Components (Widgets Ring)","description":"# Phase 5: Layout + Components (Widgets Ring)

## Purpose
Implement the widget layer that builds on kernel primitives. Widgets are ring-2 concepts - they depend on the kernel but the kernel does not depend on them.

## Renderable Trait
The core abstraction for components:
- measure(&self, ctx: &RenderContext) -> Measurement / TextMeasurement
- render(&self, ctx: &RenderContext, rect: Rect, buffer: &mut Buffer)

## Measurement Protocol
- minimum: tightest compression width
- maximum: ideal unconstrained width
- union(): for side-by-side layout (max of both)
- stack(): for vertical stacking (sum of both)

## v1 Essential Widgets (Agent Harness Needs)

### Viewport / Log Viewer
- Scrollable text area for streaming logs
- Essential for agent harness UIs

### Panel
- Bordered container with optional title
- Border presets: Rounded, Square, Double, Heavy, Ascii, Hidden
- Title alignment: Left, Center, Right

### Spinner
- Animated progress indicator
- Styles: Dots, Line, Braille, Bounce, Custom
- Frame advancement via tick()

### Progress Bar
- Value 0.0-1.0 with optional percentage display
- Customizable filled/empty characters and colors

### Text Input / Prompt
- Single-line editable text field
- Cursor management (selection/history can be future)

### Status Line
- Fixed-position information display
- For tool indicators, status messages

## Hit Testing (Optional)
HitGrid for mouse affordances - maps screen positions to interactive elements.

## Exit Criteria
- Snapshot tests for all widgets across themes and sizes
- Hit testing works where applicable
- Widgets compose correctly via Renderable trait

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Harness-essential widgets exist and are tested (unit + snapshot + PTY where needed).
- [ ] Widgets build only on kernel primitives (no hidden writes; no kernel deps on widgets).
- [ ] Layout/measurement behavior is deterministic and documented.","notes":"## Widget Trait Design Decision

The Renderable trait should include RenderBudget via RenderContext:

```rust
pub struct RenderContext<'a> {
    /// Available render budget for this frame
    pub budget: &'a RenderBudget,
    /// Terminal capabilities for feature detection
    pub capabilities: &'a TerminalCapabilities,
    /// Current theme
    pub theme: &'a Theme,
    /// Clip region (for scissor stacks)
    pub clip: Option<Rect>,
}

pub trait Renderable {
    /// Measure the widget's size requirements
    fn measure(&self, ctx: &RenderContext) -> Measurement;
    
    /// Render to buffer within the given rect
    fn render(&self, ctx: &RenderContext, rect: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer);
}
```

This design:
1. Keeps the trait signature clean (2 params + buf)
2. Makes budget-awareness automatic for all widgets
3. Allows future context extensions without trait changes
4. Context is borrowed, so no allocation

All widget implementations should use `ctx.budget.degradation()` to check degradation level.

This should be reflected in all widget implementations (bd-128s et al).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:06:02.781831931Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T05:21:23.721028862Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-10i.7","depends_on_id":"bd-10i","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:06:02.781831931Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-10i.7","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:21:21.822401345Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-10i.7","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:21:21.560738568Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-10i.8","title":"Phase 6: Runtime + Agent Harness Reference App","description":"# Phase 6: Runtime + Agent Harness Reference App

## Purpose
Implement the optional-but-recommended Bubbletea/Elm-style runtime and build a reference agent harness app that proves the architecture works for its primary use case.

## Runtime Model (Bubbletea/Elm Lineage)

### Model Trait
- init() -> Cmd: initialization commands
- update(msg) -> Cmd: state transitions
- view(&self, frame: &mut Frame): render current state

### Cmd Enum
- None: no-op
- Quit: exit application
- Batch(Vec<Cmd>): parallel commands
- Sequence(Vec<Cmd>): sequential commands
- Msg(M): send message to self
- Tick(Duration): schedule tick
- Async integration (feature-gated): threadpool or tokio

### Program Responsibilities
- Own terminal lifecycle (raw mode, mouse, paste, focus, alt screen)
- Poll input -> Event -> Message
- Run update loop, schedule ticks/commands
- Render frames only when dirty
- Enforce screen mode policy (Inline/AltScreen)
- FPS cap and frame coalescing

## Deterministic Simulator (Stop regressions forever)
ProgramSimulator that:
- Injects sequence of Events/Messages
- Captures produced Frames
- Enables snapshot tests for widgets and full apps
- Runs without real terminal (no flakiness)

## Agent Harness Reference App
The design forcing function - not 'just an example':
- Inline scrollback log stream + UI region
- Tool indicators, status line, input area
- Streaming render updates
- No flicker, no corruption
- Survives resize
- Exits cleanly on panic

## What 'Agent-Ergonomic' Means in Practice
- Hello world harness in <200 LOC
- Tool output streaming is one function call
- Pinned bottom UI is one config option
- Temporary full-screen modal supported without rewriting app

## Exit Criteria
- Harness demo passes PTY tests
- Inline mode stable under sustained output
- Simulator enables deterministic testing

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Runtime enables the "pit of success" (easy inline harness with strong defaults).
- [ ] One-writer routing is practical (LogSink + PTY capture paths exist).
- [ ] Sanitization is enforced by default for untrusted output.
- [ ] Simulator produces stable snapshots and prevents regressions.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:06:20.392811659Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-31T23:27:04.824958170Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-10i.8","depends_on_id":"bd-10i","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:06:20.392811659Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-10i.8","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:21:22.065119348Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-2wi","title":"Implement Grid layout for 2D positioning","description":"PHASE 5 - GRID LAYOUT

## Background
Grid layout provides 2D positioning with rows/columns. More powerful than Flex for dashboard-style layouts with multiple regions.

## Technical Requirements
- Grid struct with rows × cols dimensions
- Cell addressing: grid[(row, col)]
- Column/row constraints (reuse Flex constraint types)
- Cell spanning: colspan/rowspan for merged cells
- Gap between cells (row_gap, col_gap)
- Area naming: grid.area('sidebar') returns Rect
- Template syntax for declarative grid definition

## Design Rationale
CSS Grid concepts adapted for TUI. Area naming enables semantic layout references. Cell spanning handles common dashboard patterns.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Grid struct with constraint-based sizing
- [ ] Row and column constraint solving
- [ ] Cell span support (colspan, rowspan)
- [ ] Named area definition and lookup
- [ ] Gap configuration
- [ ] Template DSL for grid definition
- [ ] Overflow handling for too-small grids
- [ ] Property tests for area calculations

## Performance Budget
- Grid solve for 10×10: < 500µs
- Area lookup: O(1) after solve

 {"id":"bd-2wo","title":"Implement tmux/screen passthrough and mux detection","description":"# Implement tmux/screen Passthrough and Mux Detection

## Background
Plan Section 9.4 and ADR-001 describe multiplexer (tmux/screen/zellij) detection and passthrough handling. This is important for inline mode to work correctly under multiplexers.

## Why This Matters
- tmux/screen intercept escape sequences
- Some features (sync output, OSC 8) need passthrough
- Scroll regions behave differently under muxes
- Detection enables correct fallback strategies

## Technical Design

### Detection
```rust
impl TerminalCapabilities {
    pub fn detect() -> Self {
        // Multiplexer detection
        let in_tmux = std::env::var("TMUX").is_ok();
        let in_screen = std::env::var("STY").is_ok();
        let in_zellij = std::env::var("ZELLIJ").is_ok();
        
        // Note: WezTerm/Kitty built-in muxes don't need special handling
        
        Self {
            in_tmux,
            in_screen,
            in_zellij,
            // ...
        }
    }
    
    pub fn in_any_mux(&self) -> bool {
        self.in_tmux || self.in_screen || self.in_zellij
    }
}
```

### tmux Passthrough Wrapper
```rust
/// Wrap sequence for tmux passthrough
/// tmux intercepts escape sequences unless wrapped in DCS passthrough
pub fn tmux_wrap(sequence: &str) -> String {
    if in_tmux() {
        // Double escapes for tmux
        let escaped = sequence.replace('\\x1b', "\\x1b\\x1b");
        format\!("\\x1bPtmux;{}\\x1b\\\\", escaped)
    } else {
        sequence.to_string()
    }
}

// Usage example for OSC 8 hyperlinks under tmux
fn emit_hyperlink(url: &str) -> String {
    let osc = format\!("\\x1b]8;;{}\\x1b\\\\", url);
    tmux_wrap(&osc)
}
```

### Mux-Aware Feature Policies
```rust
impl TerminalCapabilities {
    /// Returns whether sync output should be enabled
    pub fn use_sync_output(&self) -> bool {
        // Disable in multiplexers (passthrough unreliable)
        if self.in_any_mux() {
            return false;
        }
        self.sync_output
    }
    
    /// Returns whether scroll-region optimization is safe
    pub fn use_scroll_region(&self) -> bool {
        // Conservative in muxes due to quirks
        if self.in_any_mux() {
            return false;  // Fall back to overlay redraw
        }
        self.scroll_region
    }
    
    /// OSC 8 hyperlink support
    pub fn use_hyperlinks(&self) -> bool {
        // tmux passthrough needed for hyperlinks
        // Only enable if passthrough is reliable
        if self.in_tmux {
            // Modern tmux (3.3+) supports OSC 8 passthrough
            // but detection is fragile
            return false;  // Conservative default
        }
        self.osc8_hyperlinks
    }
}
```

### Screen Passthrough
```rust
/// screen uses different passthrough syntax
pub fn screen_wrap(sequence: &str) -> String {
    if in_screen() {
        // screen uses P escape sequence
        format\!("\\x1bP{}\\x1b\\\\", sequence)
    } else {
        sequence.to_string()
    }
}
```

### Zellij Handling
```rust
// Zellij generally has better passthrough than tmux/screen
// but some features still need care
impl TerminalCapabilities {
    pub fn zellij_passthrough_mode(&self) -> bool {
        // Zellij 0.39+ has improved passthrough
        self.in_zellij
    }
}
```

## Integration Points

1. **TerminalCapabilities::detect()** - Detect multiplexer presence
2. **Presenter output** - Apply passthrough wrapping where needed
3. **ADR-001 inline mode** - Fall back to overlay redraw in muxes
4. **Feature policies** - Conservative defaults in mux environments

## Tests Required
- [ ] TMUX env var detection
- [ ] STY env var detection (screen)
- [ ] ZELLIJ env var detection
- [ ] tmux_wrap produces correct output
- [ ] screen_wrap produces correct output
- [ ] Feature policies respect mux detection
- [ ] PTY test under tmux (if CI supports)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Mux detection in TerminalCapabilities
- [ ] in_any_mux() helper method
- [ ] Passthrough wrappers for tmux/screen
- [ ] Feature policies mux-aware
- [ ] Documentation of mux limitations

 {"id":"bd-2x0j","title":"Implement property tests for diff correctness and presenter roundtrip","description":"# Implement Property Tests for Diff and Presenter Correctness

## Background
Plan Section 13.1 and J specifically require property tests to ensure diff correctness and presenter output validation. Property tests use randomized inputs to find edge cases humans miss.

## Property Test Categories

### Category 1: Diff Correctness
**Property**: Applying diff changes to old buffer produces new buffer

```rust
proptest! {
    #[test]
    fn diff_apply_produces_target(
        width in 10u16..200,
        height in 5u16..100,
        changes in proptest::collection::vec(
            (0u16..200, 0u16..100, any::<Cell>()), 
            0..1000
        )
    ) {
        // Create old buffer
        let mut old = Buffer::new(width, height);
        
        // Apply random changes to create new buffer
        let mut new = old.clone();
        for (x, y, cell) in &changes {
            if *x < width && *y < height {
                new.set(*x, *y, cell.clone());
            }
        }
        
        // Compute diff
        let diff = BufferDiff::compute(&old, &new);
        
        // Apply diff to old should produce new
        let mut result = old.clone();
        for (x, y) in diff.changes() {
            let cell = new.get(*x, *y).unwrap().clone();
            result.set(*x, *y, cell);
        }
        
        // Verify buffers match
        for y in 0..height {
            for x in 0..width {
                prop_assert_eq!(
                    result.get(x, y),
                    new.get(x, y),
                    "Mismatch at ({}, {})", x, y
                );
            }
        }
    }
}
```

### Category 2: Presenter Roundtrip
**Property**: Presenter output applied to terminal model matches source buffer

```rust
proptest! {
    #[test]
    fn presenter_roundtrip_correct(
        width in 10u16..80,
        height in 5u16..40,
        fill_ratio in 0.0f32..1.0,
    ) {
        // Create random buffer
        let buffer = generate_random_buffer(width, height, fill_ratio);
        
        // Present to bytes
        let mut output = Vec::new();
        let mut presenter = Presenter::new(&mut output, TerminalCapabilities::basic());
        presenter.present_full(&buffer, &GraphemePool::new(), &LinkRegistry::new())
            .unwrap();
        
        // Apply to terminal model
        let mut model = TerminalModel::new(width, height);
        model.apply_output(&output).unwrap();
        
        // Verify match
        prop_assert!(
            model.matches(&buffer, &GraphemePool::new()),
            "Presenter output doesn't reproduce buffer"
        );
    }
}
```

### Category 3: Style State Never Leaks
**Property**: After complete frame, style is reset

```rust
proptest! {
    #[test]
    fn style_state_reset_after_present(
        width in 10u16..80,
        height in 5u16..40,
    ) {
        let buffer = generate_styled_buffer(width, height);
        
        let mut output = Vec::new();
        let mut presenter = Presenter::new(&mut output, TerminalCapabilities::basic());
        presenter.present_full(&buffer, &GraphemePool::new(), &LinkRegistry::new())
            .unwrap();
        
        // Output must end with SGR 0 (reset)
        let output_str = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output);
        prop_assert!(
            output_str.ends_with("\x1b[0m") || 
            output_str.contains("\x1b[0m\x1b["),  // Reset before final positioning
            "Style not reset at end: {}", &output_str[output_str.len().saturating_sub(50)..]
        );
    }
}
```

### Category 4: Links Properly Closed
**Property**: Every OSC 8 link open has corresponding close

```rust
proptest! {
    #[test]
    fn links_balanced(width in 10u16..80, height in 5u16..40) {
        let buffer = generate_buffer_with_links(width, height);
        
        let mut output = Vec::new();
        let mut presenter = Presenter::new(&mut output, TerminalCapabilities { 
            osc8_hyperlinks: true, 
            ..TerminalCapabilities::basic() 
        });
        presenter.present_full(&buffer, &GraphemePool::new(), &links())
            .unwrap();
        
        let output_str = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output);
        
        // Count link opens and closes
        let opens = output_str.matches("\x1b]8;;").count()  // link with URL
            + output_str.matches("\x1b]8;id=").count();     // link with id
        let closes = output_str.matches("\x1b]8;;\x1b\\").count();
        
        // Every open needs a close (or one final close)
        prop_assert!(
            opens <= closes,
            "Unbalanced links: {} opens, {} closes", opens, closes
        );
    }
}
```

### Category 5: Width Invariants
**Property**: Rendering never writes beyond buffer bounds

```rust
proptest! {
    #[test]
    fn render_within_bounds(
        width in 10u16..80,
        height in 5u16..40,
        text in ".*",
    ) {
        let mut buffer = Buffer::new(width, height);
        
        // Render text into buffer (via whatever rendering API)
        render_text(&mut buffer, &text, 0, 0, width);
        
        // Check no writes beyond bounds
        // (Buffer should enforce this internally, but verify)
        for y in 0..height {
            let mut x = 0;
            while x < width {
                let cell = buffer.get(x, y).unwrap();
                let cell_width = cell.width() as u16;
                
                // Wide char can't overflow right edge
                prop_assert!(
                    x + cell_width <= width,
                    "Wide char at ({}, {}) overflows (width {})",
                    x, y, cell_width
                );
                
                x += cell_width.max(1);
            }
        }
    }
}
```

## Test Helpers Needed
- `generate_random_buffer(w, h, fill_ratio)` - Random content
- `generate_styled_buffer(w, h)` - Random styles
- `generate_buffer_with_links(w, h)` - Random hyperlinks
- `Arbitrary` impl for Cell, CellContent, CellAttrs, PackedRgba

## Running Property Tests
```bash
# Run with many iterations
PROPTEST_CASES=10000 cargo test --test prop_tests

# Generate regression cases on failure
PROPTEST_ENABLE_FAILURE_PERSISTENCE=1 cargo test
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Diff apply property test passes 10k iterations
- [ ] Presenter roundtrip property test passes 10k iterations
- [ ] Style reset property test passes 10k iterations
- [ ] Link balance property test passes 10k iterations
- [ ] Width bounds property test passes 10k iterations
- [ ] No panics under property testing
- [ ] Regression cases saved for failed tests

-{"id":"bd-2yd","title":"Implement StyleSheet registry for named styles","description":"PHASE 4 - STYLESHEET REGISTRY

## Background
StyleSheet provides named style registration similar to CSS classes. Enables theme-able applications and consistent style reuse without hardcoding colors.

## Technical Requirements
- StyleSheet struct with HashMap<StyleId, Style>
- StyleId: either interned string or u32 index
- Register named styles: stylesheet.define('error', Style::new().fg(RED).bold())
- Lookup by name: stylesheet.get('error')
- Style composition: stylesheet.compose(['base', 'error']) merges in order
- Default styles for common semantic names (error, warning, info, success, muted)

## Design Rationale
Named styles decouple visual appearance from widget logic. Themes can override the stylesheet without changing widget code. Matches patterns from Textual/Rich.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] StyleSheet with define/get/compose API
- [ ] StyleId type (string or integer based on perf analysis)
- [ ] Thread-safe for read access (RwLock or immutable after init)
- [ ] Default semantic style set
- [ ] Style merging for composition
- [ ] Clear error handling for missing styles

## Integration Points
- Widgets reference styles by name
- Theme loading populates stylesheet
- Buffer.set_style() accepts Style or StyleId

 {"id":"bd-2yu","title":"Implement run grouping and style-run coalescing in Presenter","description":"# Implement Run Grouping and Style-Run Coalescing in Presenter

## Background
ADR-002 and Plan Section 8.1 specify that the Presenter must emit "grouped runs" rather than per-cell cursor moves. This is critical for minimal ANSI output and flicker reduction.

## Why This Matters
Without run grouping:
- Each changed cell requires a cursor move: `\x1b[y;xH`
- Each cell change might reset style: `\x1b[0m...`
- Output is O(changes × escape_overhead)
- Slow terminals show visible tearing

With run grouping:
- Consecutive changed cells emit one cursor move
- Style only changes when cell style differs
- Output is O(runs × escape_overhead)
- Smooth, fast rendering

## Technical Design

### ChangeRun Structure
```rust
/// A contiguous run of changed cells on a single row
pub struct ChangeRun {
    pub y: u16,   // Row
    pub x0: u16,  // Start column (inclusive)
    pub x1: u16,  // End column (inclusive)
}
```

### Run Grouping Algorithm
```rust
impl BufferDiff {
    /// Convert point changes into row-major runs
    pub fn runs(&self, width: u16) -> Vec<ChangeRun> {
        if self.changes.is_empty() {
            return Vec::new();
        }
        
        // Sort by (y, x) for row-major order
        let mut sorted: Vec<_> = self.changes.iter().copied().collect();
        sorted.sort_by_key(|(x, y)| (*y, *x));
        
        let mut runs = Vec::new();
        let mut i = 0;
        
        while i < sorted.len() {
            let (x0, y) = sorted[i];
            let mut x1 = x0;
            i += 1;
            
            // Coalesce consecutive x positions on same row
            while i < sorted.len() {
                let (x, yy) = sorted[i];
                if yy != y || x != x1 + 1 {
                    break;
                }
                x1 = x;
                i += 1;
            }
            
            runs.push(ChangeRun { y, x0, x1 });
        }
        
        runs
    }
}
```

### Style-Run Coalescing
Within a run, group cells by style to minimize SGR changes:

```rust
impl Presenter {
    fn emit_run(
        &mut self,
        run: &ChangeRun,
        buffer: &Buffer,
        pool: &GraphemePool,
        links: &LinkRegistry,
    ) -> io::Result<()> {
        // Single cursor move for entire run
        self.move_cursor_to(run.x0, run.y)?;
        
        // Emit cells, tracking style changes
        for x in run.x0..=run.x1 {
            let cell = buffer.get_unchecked(x, run.y);
            if cell.is_continuation() {
                continue;  // Skip wide char continuations
            }
            
            // Only emit style change if different from current
            self.emit_style_if_changed(cell)?;
            self.emit_link_if_changed(cell, links)?;
            self.emit_content(cell, pool)?;
            
            // Cursor advances naturally after character output
            self.cursor_x += cell.width() as u16;
        }
        
        Ok(())
    }
    
    fn emit_style_if_changed(&mut self, cell: &Cell) -> io::Result<()> {
        let new_style = CellStyle {
            fg: cell.fg,
            bg: cell.bg,
            attrs: cell.attrs.flags(),
        };
        
        if Some(new_style) == self.current_style {
            return Ok(());  // No change needed
        }
        
        // Emit minimal SGR change
        // v1: reset+apply (simple, correct)
        // future: incremental (fewer bytes)
        self.writer.write_all(b"\x1b[0")?;
        // ... emit style codes ...
        self.writer.write_all(b"m")?;
        self.current_style = Some(new_style);
        
        Ok(())
    }
}
```

### Optimization Opportunities
1. **Skip cursor move when at expected position** - After emitting a run, cursor may be at next change
2. **Batch style attributes** - Emit `\x1b[0;1;38;2;r;g;bm` not separate sequences
3. **Link run grouping** - Keep link open across cells with same link_id
4. **Empty cell optimization** - Runs of spaces can use `\x1b[nX` (erase characters)

## Performance Targets
- Cursor moves reduced by 50-90% vs naive
- Style changes reduced by 30-70%
- Bytes per frame for "small changes" is O(changes), not O(screen)

## Tests Required
- [ ] Empty diff produces no runs
- [ ] Single cell change produces one run
- [ ] Consecutive cells on row produce one run
- [ ] Non-consecutive cells produce multiple runs
- [ ] Cross-row changes produce separate runs
- [ ] Style tracking minimizes SGR output
- [ ] Link tracking minimizes OSC 8 output
- [ ] Benchmark: bytes emitted vs naive approach

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] BufferDiff::runs() implemented
- [ ] Presenter uses runs for emission
- [ ] Style-run coalescing active
- [ ] Link-run coalescing active
- [ ] Benchmark shows byte reduction
- [ ] PTY tests verify correct output

 {"id":"bd-2zd","title":"Implement scroll-region optimization for inline mode","description":"PHASE 2.5 - SCROLL REGION OPTIMIZATION

## Background
Scroll regions (DECSTBM) can optimize inline mode by pinning the UI region and letting logs scroll naturally. This is an OPTIMIZATION, not the baseline.

## Technical Requirements

### DECSTBM Usage
- Set scroll region to exclude UI: ESC[1;{log_bottom}r
- Logs scroll within region, UI stays pinned
- Must restore scroll region on exit

### Capability Gating
Only use when:
- caps.scroll_region == true
- NOT in tmux/screen/zellij (mux passthrough issues)
- Terminal known to support correctly

### Implementation

pub struct ScrollRegionPresenter {
    ui_height: u16,
    scroll_region_active: bool,
}

impl ScrollRegionPresenter {
    pub fn setup_scroll_region(&mut self, writer: &mut TerminalWriter) {
        if !self.can_use_scroll_region() {
            return;  // Fall back to overlay
        }
        
        let (_, height) = writer.size();
        let log_bottom = height - self.ui_height;
        
        // Set scroll region (excludes UI at bottom)
        write!(writer, "\x1b[1;{}r", log_bottom)?;
        self.scroll_region_active = true;
    }
    
    pub fn restore_scroll_region(&mut self, writer: &mut TerminalWriter) {
        if self.scroll_region_active {
            writer.write_all(b"\x1b[r")?;  // Reset to full screen
            self.scroll_region_active = false;
        }
    }
    
    fn can_use_scroll_region(&self) -> bool {
        let caps = TerminalCapabilities::detect();
        caps.scroll_region && 
        !caps.in_tmux &&
        !caps.in_screen &&
        !caps.in_zellij
    }
}

### Mux Detection
- TMUX env var presence
- TERM containing "screen"
- ZELLIJ env var presence

### Resize Handling
On terminal resize:
1. Disable scroll region
2. Recalculate dimensions
3. Re-enable if still appropriate

## Tests Required
- [ ] Scroll region setup/teardown
- [ ] Logs scroll within region
- [ ] UI stays pinned at bottom
- [ ] Mux detection works
- [ ] Resize re-anchoring
- [ ] PTY test: scroll region behavior

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] DECSTBM used correctly
- [ ] Capability gating works
- [ ] Mux passthrough disabled
- [ ] Fallback to overlay works
- [ ] Clean restore on exit

 {"id":"bd-30h","title":"Implement Sparkline and Chart widgets","description":"PHASE 7 - EXTRAS: DATA VISUALIZATION

## Background
Sparkline and Chart widgets provide data visualization for metrics, trends, and timeseries. Common requirement for monitoring dashboards and CLI tools.

## Technical Requirements

### Sparkline Widget
- Compact single-line visualization
- Data as Vec<f64> or iterator
- Auto-scaling to min/max range
- Configurable bar character set (▁▂▃▄▅▆▇█)
- Color gradient based on value
- Reference line support (mean, threshold)

### Chart Widget (BarChart, LineChart)
- BarChart:
  - Horizontal/vertical orientation
  - Grouped and stacked modes
  - Labels and value display
  - Color per series
- LineChart:
  - Multiple series support
  - Point markers optional
  - Area fill under line
  - Axis labels and ticks
  - Legend positioning

## Design Rationale
Feature-gated for projects that need visualization. Sparkline is minimal overhead. Full charts use Canvas internally for rendering.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Sparkline with auto-scaling
- [ ] Sparkline color gradient
- [ ] BarChart with orientations
- [ ] BarChart grouped/stacked
- [ ] LineChart multi-series
- [ ] LineChart axis rendering
- [ ] Feature gate: charts
